rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (ProtoCAMEL's role in ensuring safe communication protocols), shows where the draft drifts from it (overstating safety properties and eliminating an entire class of failures), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one claim (the counterfactual about propagated downstream harm) but does not mark confidence or missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free but with one minor regression (presuming the counterfactual about propagated downstream harm).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are not traced.
